MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Defendant And Cross-Defendant Aaron Hulme To Appear And Testify At Deposition, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Isle Scott, Esq. (415-298-3600; iscott@nossaman.com) of Nossaman LLP for Defendants Suppenkueche, Inc. and Fabricius Wiest.
Sanjeet Ganjam, Esq. (415-421-6500; SGanjam@sflaw.com) of Shartis Friese LLP for Cross-Defendants, Eurofahrt, LLC and Aaron Hulme.
Marie Holvick, Esq. (415-486-5900; mholvick@grsm.com) of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani LLP for Defendants/Cross-Defendants, Biergarten, LLC, Eurofahrt, LLC and Aaron Hulme.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Suppenkueche, Inc.'s "motion to compel defendant and cross-defendant Aaron Hulme to appear and testify at deposition and for monetary sanctions" is granted. The judge pro tem's report and recommendations are adopted.

Hulme cites no authority to justify his failure to appear at a properly noticed deposition on the ground that another action is pending between the parties. Hulme shall appear for deposition at a time and place satisfactory to the moving party on or before July 23, 2021. Hulme and his counsel are ordered to pay moving party $10,000 in sanctions. (CCP 2023.010, 2023.030.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com, Tel: 707-305-8964. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Quash Cross-Complaintants Deposition Subpoena And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-18-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Allison Callahan (acallaghan@nossaman.com) and Patrick Richard of Nossman, LLP for cross-complainants, Suppenkueche, Inc., et al
Nick Deming (ndemding@grsm.com) of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP for cross-defendants, Biergarten, LLC, et al.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTs BIERGARTEN, LLC and EUROFAHRT, LLC's Motion To Quash Cross-Complaintants Deposition Subpoena And Request For Sanctions:

The Motion is denied. The bank statements sought are potentially relevant to determining whether Eurofahrt's indemnity obligations are triggered. To the extent that privacy rights are implicated, the need for discovery outweighs those rights. The parties are directed to enter into a protective order which limits review of the documents to attorneys' eyes only and which prohibits the use of the subpoenaed documents in any other lawsuit.

Sanctions in the amount of $2,500 are awarded to Suppenkueche and Wiest. Among other reasons, the moving party failed to reasonably meet and confer.

Prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to tom@thomascohen.com

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
